Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient admitted to the crisis triage system, including their medical status and personal details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Patient entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the patient.

lastName.description: The last name of the patient.

admissionDateTime.description: The date and time when the patient was admitted to the facility.

reportedSymptoms.description: A free-text description of the initial symptoms reported by or for the patient upon admission.

triageStatusId.description: Reference to the TriageStatus indicating the patient's current criticality level. (Relationship: TriageStatus 1:N Patient)

diagnosis.description: The current medical diagnosis or suspected condition for the patient.

currentMedicalStatus.description: The current specific medical state or action for the patient (e.g., 'Waiting for X-Ray', 'Admitted to Ward', 'Discharged').

currentDestination.description: The current physical location or department where the patient is assigned (e.g., 'Emergency Department', 'ICU', 'Operating Room').

bedNumber.description: The specific bed number assigned to the patient within their current destination, if applicable.

lastUpdatedDateTime.description: The date and time when the patient record was last updated.

TriageStatus.title: TriageStatus
TriageStatus.description: Defines the different levels of patient criticality used for triage in the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TriageStatus entity.

name.description: The name of the triage status (e.g., 'Critical', 'Urgent', 'Minor', 'Deceased').

description.description: A detailed description of the criteria associated with this triage status.

severityLevel.description: An integer representing the criticality level, where a lower number typically indicates higher severity (e.g., 1 for Critical, 4 for Minor).

BloodProduct.title: BloodProduct
BloodProduct.description: Represents an individual unit or batch of blood product available in the facility's inventory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BloodProduct entity.

bloodType.description: The ABO blood group type of the blood product (e.g., 'A', 'B', 'AB', 'O').

rhFactor.description: The Rh factor of the blood product (e.g., '+' or '-').

unitIdentifier.description: A unique identifier for this specific blood product unit (e.g., a donation ID or batch number).

volumeML.description: The volume of the blood product in milliliters.

collectionDateTime.description: The date and time when the blood product was collected.

expirationDateTime.description: The date and time when the blood product expires.

status.description: The current status of the blood product (e.g., 'Available', 'Reserved', 'Used', 'Expired', 'Discarded').

storageLocation.description: The physical location within the facility where the blood product is stored (e.g., 'Blood Bank Freezer 1').

Ventilator.title: Ventilator
Ventilator.description: Represents a medical ventilator device, its status, and current assignment to a patient.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ventilator entity.

serialNumber.description: The unique serial number identifying the specific ventilator device.

model.description: The make and model of the ventilator.

status.description: The current operational status of the ventilator (e.g., 'Available', 'In Use', 'Under Maintenance', 'Out of Service').

location.description: The current physical location of the ventilator within the facility (e.g., 'ICU', 'ED', 'Storage Room').

lastMaintenanceDateTime.description: The date and time of the last maintenance performed on the ventilator.

assignedPatientId.description: Reference to the Patient currently utilizing this ventilator. This field is null if the ventilator is not in use. (Relationship: Patient 0..1:1 Ventilator)

definition.description: A top-level collection storing individual patient records. Access to these records (create, read, update, delete) is restricted to users identified as authorized medical staff.

A top-level collection containing definitions of the various triage criticality levels. This is primarily a read-only reference data set for medical staff, with write access limited to administrators.
A top-level collection managing the inventory of blood products. Access is restricted to authorized medical staff who manage inventory.
A top-level collection tracking the status and assignment of medical ventilators. Access is restricted to authorized medical staff responsible for equipment management.

A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at this path for a given `userId` (corresponding to `request.auth.uid`) designates that user as an authorized medical staff member. This is used to grant general application access and read/write privileges to core data collections. (Assumes an implicit 'User' entity for schema reference).

A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at this path for a given `userId` designates that user as an administrator. Administrators have elevated privileges, typically including all medical staff privileges plus the ability to manage reference data (e.g., TriageStatus) and user roles. (Assumes an implicit 'User' entity for schema reference).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the provided core design principles and strategy mandates. It leverages top-level collections for each primary entity: 'Patient', 'TriageStatus', 'BloodProduct', and 'Ventilator'.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** Authorization is achieved through a Database Access Control (DBAC) model based on explicit user roles stored in dedicated collections, not through hierarchical dependencies or `get()` calls in security rules. We introduce two global role collections: `/medical_staff/{userId}` and `/admins/{userId}`. The mere existence of a document at `medical_staff/$(request.auth.uid)` grants medical staff privileges, and similarly for `admins`. This completely decouples data access from specific document hierarchies within the main data collections, fulfilling the Authorization Independence mandate. For example, to access any patient data, the rule will only need to check the user's role document, like `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/medical_staff/$(request.auth.uid))`, ensuring atomic operations and easy debugging.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** Each primary data collection (`/patients`, `/triage_statuses`, `/blood_products`, `/ventilators`) is designed with a homogeneous security posture. All documents within these collections share the same access requirements (e.g., all patient records are accessible to authorized medical staff). This structural segregation ensures that `list` operations can be performed securely and efficiently. Security rules will simply grant or deny access to the entire collection based on the user's role, without needing to filter documents within the rules themselves. This prevents costly and insecure full-collection scans during `list` operations. For instance, any authorized medical staff can list all patients, and the rules will enforce this by checking the user's role document, not by inspecting each patient document's content.

**Data Relationships:** Relationships like `Patient` to `TriageStatus` (`triageStatusId`) and `Ventilator` to `Patient` (`assignedPatientId`) are implemented via direct document references (IDs), not nested subcollections. This maintains flexibility and avoids unnecessary deep hierarchies that can complicate queries and security rules.

**DBAC Implementation:** By using `/medical_staff/{userId}` and `/admins/{userId}` based on 'Existence over Content', role assignment and checking become extremely simple and efficient for security rules. This strategy completely avoids custom claims and hard-to-manage rule logic.

Regarding the user's specific request: "เมื่อกดปุ่ม เพิ่มผู้ป่วยใหม่ ต้องสร้างหน้าใหม่ไม่ใช่แสดงเป็น Popup ออกมาและ ไม่ต้องมี AI แนะนำระดับการคัดกรอง" - The proposed data structure fully supports adding new patient records via the `/patients/{patientId}` collection. The UI/UX preference for a new page over a popup and the decision to exclude AI triage suggestions do not impact the database schema itself, but the `Patient` entity's `triageStatusId` can be manually updated as required.

Finally, the proposed structure is compatible with the 'er-triage-system' Project ID as Firestore configurations are per-project and independent of the data structure design.
